h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner continues following the tutor's placing and reading instructions without reinterpretation.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner follows the tutor's step-by-step instructions without independently enacting a new device or criterion.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No learner pressure triggers a tutor strategy change; tutoring remains consistent.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: No adaptive mechanism is needed or enacted.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn, so no contingent uptake.

learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner follows the tutor's step-by-step instructions without independently enacting a new device or criterion.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot learner turn, so no contingent uptake.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: No learner pressure triggers a tutor strategy change; tutoring remains consistent.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: No learner pressure triggers a tutor strategy change; tutoring remains consistent.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: No adaptive mechanism is needed or enacted.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue to label the forces as instructed, without questioning the diagram's conditions.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: If there is no rub mark on the diagram, does the friction card stay off?
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner applies the learned distinction (friction requires surface interaction) to a hypothetical new condition, performing a productive action beyond mere acceptance.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The transcript ends after L3, so no later tutor turn exists to take stock or invent an adaptive mechanism.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn exists, so no contingent adaptation occurs.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: If there is no rub mark on the diagram, does the friction card stay off?
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner applies the learned distinction (friction requires surface interaction) to a hypothetical new condition, performing a productive action beyond mere acceptance.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot learner turn exists, so no contingent adaptation occurs.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The transcript ends after L3, so no later tutor turn exists to take stock or invent an adaptive mechanism.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The transcript ends after L3, so no later tutor turn exists to take stock or invent an adaptive mechanism.

scored.recoheredEarlier: The 4 still pulls toward speed for me, so I want to test it by putting the bar line after the third tile.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: so here the 4 is naming that unit, not setting speed.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would identify which box the bar line tested and label the numbers as instructed.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: The pencil marks one bar in 3/8: three eighth-note beat units. The quarter-note tile covers two eighth-note units, so it fills two counts and leaves one eighth-note unit left of the bar line.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully uses the bar line device in a new context and accurately interprets the relationship between note values and beat units.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The pencil has made one bar. Which box did the bar line test: the three-beat box or the quarter-note-tile box? Say it from the tiles; then we label top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor introduces a concrete bar line test to address the learner's speed misconception, but does not explicitly contrast with a failed prior approach, so it is a competent but unmarked adaptation.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit. Read the lower box first: with 8 here, which card is the one beat unit? Then we ask how many of that unit the quarter-note tile covers, not what speed it sets.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The tutor’s device of the bar line test and the consistent mantra gives the learner a precise, physically grounded criterion to distinguish the function of time signature numbers, directly countering the speed misconception.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit. Read the lower box first: with 8 here, which card is the one beat unit? Then we ask how many of that unit the quarter-note tile covers, not what speed it sets.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor immediately takes up the learner's proposed test case (3/8) and extends the established bar-line device, thereby adapting the lesson to the learner's revised framing.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: The tutor’s device of the bar line test and the consistent mantra gives the learner a precise, physically grounded criterion to distinguish the function of time signature numbers, directly countering the speed misconception.
